_documentation: Configuration for scripts/sync-board.py. See docs/board-bios-setup.md for the step-by-step on creating the Google Form, linking it to a Sheet, and filling in the URLs below. While `csv_url` is empty, the workflow runs but no-ops cleanly — so the workflow stays green while the Form is still being set up.
_columns_doc: Header names exactly as Google Sheets writes them for this form. Timestamp + email columns are localised to the form owner's account language (this form is owned by an FR-locale account, so the timestamp column is 'Horodateur' and email is 'Adresse e-mail'). If you ever rename a question in the form, mirror the change here so the sync script keeps matching.

columns.name: Full name (with title — Dr / Prof / Mr / Ms)
columns.role: What is your role at EISS?
columns.position: Position or current role (e.g. PhD candidate, Associate Professor, Policy analyst)
columns.workingGroups: Working Group involvement (tick all that apply)
columns.consent: I consent to publication of my bio on eiss-europa.com

_roles_doc: Allowed values for the 'Your role' Form dropdown. The Form question's options must exactly match the `label` values below. `kind` determines which output section the entry lands in (board → /board.html — split into 'Leadership' for tier < 100 and 'Board Members' for tier ≥ 100; support → 'Support Staff' section). `tier` is the primary sort key — lower tiers appear higher within their section. Within a tier, entries sort alphabetical by surname. To add a new role: append to this list AND add the matching option to the Form dropdown.
_functional_responsibilities_doc: Allowed values for the new 'Functional responsibility' Form dropdown. The script copies whichever value the member picks into the entry's `functionalResponsibility` field verbatim; the template renders it as a quiet pill alongside the primary role. The list here exists only as a sanity check on the Form options — if a member submits an unrecognised value, the script accepts it (forward-compatible) but logs a warning.
